content:
2 min read
Just now

--

Saw this on Overheard Sales Instagram account last month and laughed out loud: https://www.instagram.com/p/DZXmhGgRWFu/
Then I realized: they ARE an AI-ready company.
They just don’t know it yet.
Every SMB Has an Alice
Most SMBs run exactly like this:

• One critical spreadsheet

• One person who actually knows what’s in it

• Everyone else just asks them
That person is Alice. She’s the ops manager. Or the office manager. Or the founder’s assistant. Or the founder herself.
Alice’s Instinct Is Right (And Her Read Is Wrong)
When the AE calls, Alice’s instinct is: “Don’t touch my spreadsheet. It’s working.”
That instinct is 100% correct. The spreadsheet is the source of truth.
Ripping it out and replacing it with a “modern AI-native SaaS” would be a disaster.
But her READ on AI is wrong.
She’s assuming “AI” means “replace what I built.” What AI actually means is “extend what you built.”
The Spreadsheet IS Your AI Starting Point
Alice’s spreadsheet is the perfect starting point for AI. It has three things AI needs:
1. Structure: Alice designed columns and tabs the way her business actually works. That’s the data model. Nobody has to reverse-engineer it.
2. Context: Alice knows what every column means, why every rule exists, and where every exception is buried. That’s the business logic. Nobody has to guess at it.
3. Truth: The spreadsheet has been running the business. Every entry has been checked in the real world. It’s not theoretical data. It’s real.
How to Actually use AI on this spreadsheet
1. Have Alice describe it: Every column. Every weird convention. Every “we ignore the 2019 rows because of that time we changed CRMs.” Write it down.
2. Upload everything in an AI tool: Upload the spreadsheet and context file (Alice’s description) into ChatGPT, Claude or Love Spreadsheets
3. Automate: Ask the AI deep questions. Ask to schedule reports. Ask for dashboards.
The AI brings speed. Alice brings the context AI doesn’t have. The spreadsheet brings the truth.
That’s the model. Not “rip and replace.” Extend and amplify.
